uncertainties.read: Field-split Schedule A sheet: only the 'Type of Income' block (Block C) is present. Printed income columns left-to-right: EIF, None, Dividends, Rent, Interest, Capital Gains, Excepted/Blind Trust, Tax Deferred, and a far-right 'Partnership Income' column. Value (Block B), Amount of Income (Block D) and Transaction (Block E) blocks are on separate sheets and are nulled here. NEW trust header 'M&R Trust Partnership' (distinct from the Grandchildren's Trust of pages 154-160 and the Ritu Ahuja 1995 Trust). Asset slice: BROAD STREET SENIOR CREDIT PARTNERS II through WEST STREET ENERGY PARTNERS (mixed private funds + GS structured notes). Merge by asset name with the matching Value / Amount / Transaction sheets for this same M&R Trust Partnership slice. 'PARTNERSHIP INCOME' recorded via other_income_spec since it is a form-specific write-in column not in the standard income_types enum.

Rows 1,3,5,6,7,15,20,31 (BROAD STREET, FIDELIS, both GLOBAL LONG SHORT, GLOBAL PRIVATE OPPORTUNITIES, HEDGE FUND SELECT, SMS, WEST STREET CAPITAL): both the None column and the far-right Partnership Income column appear checked (X). Recorded income_types=['NONE'] plus other_income_spec='PARTNERSHIP INCOME' verbatim; the None+Partnership co-occurrence is what is printed and may reflect a form quirk (None = no current-year distributions on a partnership-type asset).

ODM INVESTORS LP (a) (liquidated in 2018): EIF checked. Second mark read as DIVIDENDS based on its horizontal position in the BL crop (~Dividends column offset); could plausibly be NONE for a liquidated fund. Lower confidence.
U S DOLLAR: single mark read as INTEREST (position near the Interest column on full page); could be Rent-adjacent but Interest is the semantically expected type for a cash/USD holding.
